title: Failure to copy DockerV2Schema2MediaType from oci folder
body:
skopeo version tested: 1.14.5, built from HEAD on 2025-04-11 (c5ccf21893f8a1c2da5709c7b425c4854992a2b8)

When a DockerV2Schema2MediaType image is pulled to an oci folder using --preserve-digests (to not invalidate image signature), when we try to copy this same image back to an OCI repostory, skopeo fails with the error message `unsupported manifest MIME types ["application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json"]`

Here is an example:

```
skopeo copy --preserve-digests docker://docker.io/library/nginx:1.13 oci:/oci-pull:nginx:1.13
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4a99993b8636 done   |
Copying blob f2aa67a397c4 done   |
Copying blob 3c091c23e29d done   |
Copying config ae513a4784 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination

skopeo copy --preserve-digests oci:/oci-pull:nginx:1.13 docker://core-harbor.apps.mydomain.com/test/nginx:1.13
FATA[0000] initializing source oci:/oci-pull:nginx:1.13: reference "nginx:1.13" matches unsupported manifest MIME types ["application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json"]

oras manifest fetch docker.io/library/nginx:1.13
{
   "schemaVersion": 2,
   "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json",
 ...
```
This seems like an arbitrary limitation because if I remove it with this quick and dirty patch:

```
diff --git a/vendor/github.com/containers/image/v5/oci/layout/oci_transport.go b/vendor/github.com/containers/image/v5/oci/layout/oci_transport.go
index 6ab38467..f2e691ee 100644
--- a/vendor/github.com/containers/image/v5/oci/layout/oci_transport.go
+++ b/vendor/github.com/containers/image/v5/oci/layout/oci_transport.go
@@ -12,6 +12,7 @@ import (
        "github.com/containers/image/v5/directory/explicitfilepath"
        "github.com/containers/image/v5/docker/reference"
        "github.com/containers/image/v5/internal/image"
+       "github.com/containers/image/v5/internal/manifest"
        "github.com/containers/image/v5/oci/internal"
        "github.com/containers/image/v5/transports"
        "github.com/containers/image/v5/types"
@@ -234,7 +235,7 @@ func (ref ociReference) getManifestDescriptor() (imgspecv1.Descriptor, int, erro
                var unsupportedMIMETypes []string
                for i, md := range index.Manifests {
                        if refName, ok := md.Annotations[imgspecv1.AnnotationRefName]; ok && refName == ref.image {
-                               if md.MediaType == imgspecv1.MediaTypeImageManifest || md.MediaType == imgspecv1.MediaTypeImageIndex {
+                               if md.MediaType == imgspecv1.MediaTypeImageManifest || md.MediaType == imgspecv1.MediaTypeImageIndex || md.MediaType == manifest.DockerV2Schema2MediaType {
                                        return md, i, nil
                                }
                                unsupportedMIMETypes = append(unsupportedMIMETypes, md.MediaType)

```

Then it works:

```
/skopeo copy --preserve-digests oci:/oci-pull:nginx:1.13 docker://core-harbor.apps.mydomain.com/test/nginx:1.13
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f2aa67a397c4 done   |
Copying blob 3c091c23e29d done   |
Copying blob 4a99993b8636 done   |
Copying config ae513a4784 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination

$ ~/oras manifest fetch core-harbor.apps.mydomain.com/test/nginx:1.13
{
   "schemaVersion": 2,
   "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
...
```

Considering we can pull a DockerV2Schema2MediaType to an oci folder, would it be possible to remove the limitation that prevents pushing it back to a repo in that same format?

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Well, it’s not an “arbitrary” limitation in the sense that the created file structure outright violates the spec. `--preserve-digests` prevents the automatic conversion to OCI; arguably it’s a bug that this is possible, it would make sense to add a check the destination to fail even in that case.

Anyway, this has already been relaxed in https://github.com/containers/image/pull/2695 , and that will land in the next Skopeo release.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
